dataset: Admiralty NP337 Tidal Stream Atlas: Solent and Adjacent Waters (reference dataset)
case_id: Diamond J reference point, Nab Channel area

pi_items.source: Bridge Command World file specification (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/WorldFileSpec.php) - standard directory structure for world scenarios
Bridge Command World file specification (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/WorldFileSpec.php) - standard tidal stream configuration file format
Nab Tower (reference point for Nab Channel) is located at 50°40'N = 50.667°N (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nab_Tower). Metadata latitude is 0.0497° (~5.5 km) higher than actual Nab Tower position.
Nab Tower is at 0°57'W = -0.9500°W (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nab_Tower). Exact match to decimal conversion.

Southampton (nearest major tidal reference in Solent) has mean spring tidal range of 4.0m (https://www.southamptonvts.co.uk/Port_Information/Navigation/Hydrography/Southampton_Tides/). Metadata value 4.2m is 0.2m (5%) higher than documented Southampton reference.
Southampton has mean neap tidal range of 1.9m (https://www.southamptonvts.co.uk/). Metadata value 2.1m is 0.2m (10.5%) higher than documented Southampton reference.

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly stated, but data format suggests High Water Portsmouth
pi_items.source: NP337 Tidal Stream Atlas specification confirms that Solent tidal streams are referenced to HW Portsmouth for all 13-hour charts (search result: 'NP337 atlas contains two sets of 13 charts showing tidal streams commencing 6 hours before HW Portsmouth')
Bridge Command World file specification and Admiralty NP337 atlas standard format both specify 13 charts from 6 hours before to 6 hours after high water

pi_items.metadata_value: Direction, SpeedS (springs), SpeedN (neaps) for each hour
pi_items.verified_value: Direction, SpeedS (springs), SpeedN (neaps) for each hour
pi_items.source: Bridge Command World file specification (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/WorldFileSpec.php) defines exact parameter names and structure

pi_items.verified_value: Real tidal diamond J exists in Solent area with known characteristics (3.1 knots springs, 1.6 knots neaps at HW-2), but specific coordinates for this task's location do not match standard Nab Tower position
pi_items.source: Tidal diamond information from cockpitcards.co.uk; Nab Tower position from Wikipedia. Task coordinates (50.7167°, -0.9500°) appear to be either a nearby reference point or an adjusted location not exactly at Nab Tower.

pi_items.metadata_value: Based on or inspired by Admiralty NP337 Tidal Stream Atlas
pi_items.verified_value: Data uses realistic Solent tidal stream patterns and formats, but specific numerical values are not exact matches to publicly available Admiralty reference data for this area
pi_items.source: Comparison with Southampton tidal ranges (4.0m springs, 1.9m neaps) and general Solent tidal characteristics from UKHO sources and sailing resources

privileged_info_summary: This task implements a realistic tidal stream configuration for Bridge Command's Solent world scenario. The task references the Nab Channel area (Nab Tower is at 50.667°N, 0.95°W), but the metadata specifies slightly different coordinates (50.7167°N, -0.9500°W), shifting the location approximately 5.5 km north. The tidal range values (MeanRangeSprings: 4.2m, MeanRangeNeaps: 2.1m) are slightly higher than documented Southampton references (4.0m and 1.9m respectively). The data structure and format exactly match the Bridge Command World file specification for tidal streams, with 13 hours of data (-6 to +6 relative to High Water Portsmouth). The direction values (086-092° for flood phase, 268-285° for ebb phase) and speed values are plausible for Solent tidal streams but appear to be synthetic or adjusted values rather than direct extracts from the Admiralty NP337 atlas. The task successfully implements the proper file format and structure for the Bridge Command simulator, with the specific numerical values being either approximations or data for a reference point that differs from the standard Nab Tower position.
